title: Event-capture module uses fake AI adapters in production — real Azure AI adapters not implemented
body:
## Issue description

The event-capture module (`src/modules/event-capture/`) requires three AI/ML adapters at runtime:

- `IEventFieldExtractor` — extracts event fields (title, date, time, location) from natural language text.
- `ISpeechToTextAdapter` — transcribes audio input to text.
- `IImageExtractionAdapter` — extracts event-relevant text/fields from an uploaded image.

All three adapters are currently wired with deterministic fake implementations as permanent fallbacks in `src/app.ts`:

```typescript
const extractor  = deps?.eventFieldExtractor  ?? new FakeEventFieldExtractor();
const sttAdapter = deps?.speechToTextAdapter   ?? new FakeSpeechToTextAdapter();
const imageAdapter = deps?.imageExtractionAdapter ?? new FakeImageExtractionAdapter();
```

No real Azure AI adapter implementations exist in the codebase. The `FakeEventFieldExtractor` returns hardcoded, deterministic outputs regardless of input. The fake speech-to-text and image-extraction adapters similarly return fixed stubs. This means text capture, voice capture, and image capture never produce real extracted event data in any deployed environment, including production.

The `AppDependencies` interface in `src/app.ts` accepts injected adapters, but `src/server.ts` never injects real ones:

```typescript
const app = createApp({
  ...(db ? { db } : {}),
  ...(tokenValidator ? { tokenValidator } : {}),
  // No eventFieldExtractor, speechToTextAdapter, or imageExtractionAdapter injected
});
```

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

- Implement a real `AzureOpenAIEventFieldExtractor` that calls an Azure OpenAI (or Azure AI Foundry) endpoint to extract event fields from natural language text.
- Implement a real `AzureSpeechToTextAdapter` that calls Azure Cognitive Services Speech API to transcribe audio.
- Implement a real `AzureImageExtractionAdapter` that calls Azure AI Vision (OCR / multimodal) to extract text and event fields from uploaded images.
- Each real adapter must read its configuration (endpoint URL, API key or managed identity scope) exclusively from environment variables.
- Wire the real adapters in `src/server.ts` when the corresponding environment variables are present; fall back to the fake adapters only when the variables are absent (preserving existing test and dev-without-AI behaviour).
- All real external calls must go through the existing port interfaces (`IEventFieldExtractor`, `ISpeechToTextAdapter`, `IImageExtractionAdapter`) so they remain mockable in tests.
- Infrastructure Bicep (`infra/`) must be updated to provision the required Azure AI resources and grant the Container App's managed identity the necessary roles.

## Additional notes

- Architecture rule (`.github/copilot-instructions.md`): AI/ML adapters must be wrapped behind interfaces and mocked in CI tests. Real Azure AI calls must never be made in CI test runs.
- The `IBlobStorageAdapter` / `FakeBlobStorageAdapter` for image storage has the same gap: a real Azure Blob Storage adapter using managed identity must be implemented.
- The capture pipeline service (`src/modules/event-capture/application/capture-pipeline.service.ts`) is adapter-agnostic; no changes to the pipeline itself should be required.
- Confidence thresholds and low-confidence draft handling are already defined in the capture pipeline. The real adapters must return results in the existing `ExtractedEventFields` / `ExtractionResult` shape.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Real adapters are implemented for `IEventFieldExtractor`, `ISpeechToTextAdapter`, and `IImageExtractionAdapter` using Azure AI services.
- [ ] A real `IBlobStorageAdapter` using Azure Blob Storage with managed identity is implemented.
- [ ] Real adapters are activated in `src/server.ts` when the corresponding environment variables are present.
- [ ] No real Azure AI or Blob Storage calls are made in CI; fake adapters are used when env vars are absent.
- [ ] All real adapters read configuration exclusively from environment variables (no hard-coded endpoints or keys).
- [ ] Bicep infrastructure provisions the required Azure AI resources and RBAC role assignments.
- [ ] Integration tests for the capture pipeline continue to use fake adapters and pass in CI.
- [ ] Capture API tests (`src/modules/event-capture/presentation/capture.api.test.ts`) continue to pass.
- [ ] CI passes.
